title: The Kargil War
author_or_org: Government Arts College (study note / military history summary)
strengths: Gives a sector-specific troop deployment scale and conflict end date.
limitations: Teaching material rather than a primary official record.
notes: Paraphrase: Indian forces in the Kargil-Dras sector reached close to 30,000 troops, while infiltrators were around 5,000 at the height of the conflict.

title: Written answer on Kargil casualties
author_or_org: Parliament of India / Ministry of Defence
strengths: Official Indian casualty count from a parliamentary answer.
notes: Paraphrase: 527 soldiers were killed and around 1,400 were wounded during the Kargil conflict.

title: Kargil: Perspectives and Memories
strengths: Useful reminder that civilians in the inhabited theater faced deadly shelling.
limitations: The quoted approx. 60 killed is explicitly tied to the previous three years, not cleanly to the 1999 war alone.
notes: Paraphrase: the Kargil-Drass-Batalik-Kaksar area bore the brunt of shelling, and approximately 60 people were killed over the previous three years.

title: Country report excerpts on displacement in Kargil
author_or_org: US Committee for Refugees via ecoi
strengths: Strong humanitarian evidence that the inhabited theater emptied out under shelling.
limitations: Displacement alone does not solve the civilian direct-death numerator problem.
notes: Paraphrase: fighting in the Kargil area displaced roughly 60,000-100,000 people, and Kargil and Dras were largely deserted.
